source: https://stripe.com/ (browsed live 2026-07-21, 1440px viewport — new 'HDS' design-system generation)
https://stripe.com/payments (redirect from /en-us/payments — classic Stripe marketing generation)
generation_note: Stripe is mid-migration between two design generations that coexist: the homepage runs the new 'HDS' system (hds-heading classes, weight-300 display type, #533AFD accent, flowing ribbon hero); /payments and /blog still run the classic generation (Gradient__canvas diagonal mesh hero, #635BFF/#9966FF accents, weight-500 navy titles, pill CTAs); docs runs the separate Sail product-UI system (system fonts, 905 root CSS variables). The shared DNA underneath: dark navy + blurple + multi-hue gradient spectacle on a white page.

color.palette_type: navy + blurple two-anchor palette with a full multi-hue gradient spectrum (orange/pink/purple/cyan) reserved for hero canvases and illustration

docs.note: docs shifts blurple toward a calmer indigo (#5469D4 links) and desaturates text grays; Sail exposes ~905 :root custom properties (--sail-*)

color.gradient_spectrum: hero gradients sweep orange #FFB23E → coral/pink #FF5996 → magenta → blurple #635BFF → cyan #11EFE3; the same spectrum recurs in blog illustration cards, docs' dotted-grid ornament, and 'Recent highlights' tiles (saturated cyan→blue→navy→purple)
color.surface_strategy: white page; sections alternate #FFFFFF with cool tints (#F6F9FC / #F8FAFD); full-bleed dark navy bands (#0A2540→#031323) carry stats, developer content, and enterprise messaging; gradient color is spent almost entirely on the hero canvas and illustration thumbnails
color.contrast_strategy: dark navy on white for structure; blurple strictly signals interactivity (links, CTAs, category eyebrows); light-on-dark navy sections use white text with blurple/purple glow accents; gradient text only inside the hero headline

typography.heading_font: Söhne (sohne-var, variable) — fallback 'SF Pro Display' on new pages, 'Helvetica Neue'/Arial on classic pages
typography.body_font: Söhne (sohne-var) on all marketing pages; docs use the native system-ui stack (-apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto…) at 14px
typography.mono_font: Menlo / Consolas (docs code, 13px); Source Code Pro appears in classic terminal demos

scale_observed.hero_display_new: 48px / weight 300 / lh 55.2px (1.15) / ls -0.96px — homepage h1, entire paragraph-length headline set as display type
scale_observed.section_h2_new: 32px / weight 300 / lh 35.2px / ls -0.64px — homepage sections; first clause navy #061B31, trailing clause #64748D as inline two-tone
scale_observed.docs_h1: 32px / weight 700 / lh 40px — product UI goes bold where marketing goes light
scale_observed.eyebrow_classic: 18px / weight 500 / ls +0.2px / purple #9966FF — h2 used as small purple kicker above navy titles
scale_observed.ui_text: 15px / weight 425 / ls +0.2px — Stripe's signature in-between weight for links, buttons, category labels, nav

typography.weight_discipline: the split is generational: new HDS sets giant type at featherweight 300 and lets scale carry hierarchy; classic marketing uses 500 titles + 300 body; docs use 700/400. The 425 'book-medium' variable weight for interactive text is constant across all generations
typography.tracking_rule: negative tracking scales with size (-2% at display sizes); small UI text tracks slightly open (+0.2px)
typography.signature_moves: paragraph-as-headline (whole value prop set at display size, second sentence in muted color or animated gradient); inline two-tone headings (navy clause + gray clause in one h2)

spacing.content_density: spacious on marketing (near-full-viewport hero, generous section bands), comfortable-compact on docs (14px type, 28px-tall search field)
spacing.section_rhythm: marketing sections are large color-alternating bands (~120px+ vertical padding); classic pages interleave diagonal-edged bands; docs are a dense continuous column

layout.max_content_width: ~1080-1152px centered column with generous side margins at 1440px
layout.grid: 12-col; marketing favors 3-col feature/card rows and asymmetric two-col hero (text left ~60%, visual right); docs: top nav + centered content with right-floating illustration
layout.homepage_structure: white nav → hero (left: display headline + CTA pair; right/behind: WebGL ribbon; above: live 'Global GDP running on Stripe' ticker) → dark logo bar (Nvidia, Ford, Coinbase, Google, Shopify…) → white product-tour section with phone/dashboard screenshot cards → dark navy 'backbone of global commerce' stats band with purple aurora horizon glow → segmented audience sections (enterprise / startups / SaaS) as card grids with warm-gradient thumbnails → testimonial → dark developer section with giant blurple metrics (500M+, 10K+, 150K+) → CTA band → mega footer (5-col sitemap)
layout.payments_structure: diagonal-clipped gradient hero → logo bar → alternating feature sections, each = purple eyebrow + navy title + 18px gray body + product screenshot composition → full-width customer-logo case panels (OpenAI, Slack, shopify) as dark/gradient bands → navy developer band → pricing → resources
layout.diagonal_sections: classic pages cut section boundaries and hero backgrounds on a consistent shallow diagonal (~6-10deg skew) — a Stripe trademark; the new homepage drops this for straight bands
layout.alignment_tendency: left-aligned headings and hero; cards on strict grid; docs centered column

shape.border_radius: small 4px (new-gen buttons, code chips) / 6px (cards, search, docs) / 8px occasional / pill 16.5px-height-derived on classic CTAs / 50% avatars
shape.radius_rule: the new generation squares off (4px buttons at 48px height); the classic generation rounds fully (pill 'Start now' buttons); both keep card radii small (4-6px, rarely 8px) — Stripe never goes soft/bubbly on containers
shape.border_usage: minimal — hierarchy via background tints and shadows, not strokes; 1px hairlines on secondary buttons and logo-bar dividers
shape.divider_style: section color-change or diagonal cut instead of rules

elevation.shadow_style: layered, violet-tinted, soft and large — shadows are colored with the navy/violet brand cast rather than pure black

tokens_observed.classic_recipe: the historical Stripe stack — 0 13px 27px -5px rgba(50,50,93,.25), 0 8px 16px -8px rgba(0,0,0,.3) — still visible on floating product screenshots

elevation.depth_cues: floating product screenshots at slight perspective tilt, overlapping card compositions, dark panels inset in light sections; docs tilt the test-card illustration a few degrees

iconography.style: small filled-or-duotone geometric glyphs in blurple/navy; chevron '>' suffix on nearly every text link; product icons are multi-color gradient-filled shapes

motion.philosophy: one continuous ambient hero animation per page (the gradient canvas) + numeric liveness (GDP ticker counting); UI transitions themselves stay quick and conventional
motion.easing_durations: ~150-250ms hover states; hero canvas runs continuously at low speed
motion.entrance_pattern: subtle fade/translate on scroll for section content; product screenshot compositions animate panel-by-panel
motion.signature: animated gradient text in the homepage h1 — the second sentence's color cycles through the brand spectrum (sampled mid-animation as green→blue→purple)

components.button_primary_new: #533AFD fill, white 16px/400 label, 4px radius, 48px height, chevron suffix ('Get started >')
components.button_primary_classic: pill (full-round), near-black navy #031323 fill, white 15px/425 label, compact (~30px), chevron suffix ('Start now >')
components.button_secondary: white fill + 1px border, blurple label (homepage 'Sign up with Google'); on dark bands: translucent white pill
components.nav: white bar, 15px/425 links with dropdown chevrons, logo left, 'Sign in' ghost + 'Contact sales' blurple solid right; dropdowns are white floating panels with layered shadow
components.text_link: blurple (#9966FF classic / #635BFF blog / #5469D4 docs), weight 425-500, always with '>' or '→' suffix on marketing
components.cards: white, 4-6px radius, layered violet shadow, illustration/screenshot thumbnail on top (often a warm-gradient scene), title + 14-15px weight-300 body + 'Read more >' link
components.stat_display: oversized numerals (2x, 500M+, 99.999%) in blurple or gradient fill on navy/tint panels, with small caption below
components.eyebrow_pattern: purple 18px/500 kicker above every classic section title — the classic Stripe section signature
components.code_blocks: dark navy #2A2F45 terminal panels, 13px Menlo, syntax in cyan/green/orange on dark; rounded 4-8px; floating in light sections with copy affordance
components.docs_search: 6px-radius white field with '/' keycap hint + separate 'Ask AI ✦' button
components.blog_cards: white page, blurple category eyebrow (15px/425), navy title, 48px circular author avatars with name/role meta, gradient illustration hero cards
components.footer: light, 5-column sitemap of 15px links, country selector

design_style.personality: the fintech gold standard — engineering credibility (terminals, API JSON, uptime stats) wrapped in vivid painterly gradient color; serious navy suit with an iridescent lining
design_style.genre: premium fintech/developer-platform marketing — the archetype half the SaaS industry imitates
design_style.visual_language: white pages structured by navy type and cool-tint bands; color is spent in one place per viewport — a huge multi-hue gradient canvas, a gradient illustration card, or a glowing stat — never sprinkled; product screenshots (dashboard, checkout, phone) are the primary imagery, floated with tilted perspective and violet shadows
design_style.composition: asymmetric hero (display headline left, ambient gradient canvas behind/right); alternating light/dark full-bleed bands pace the scroll like chapters; classic pages cut band edges on a shallow diagonal; feature sections pair eyebrow+title+body column with a layered screenshot composition, sides alternating
design_style.imagery: no photography of people on core pages; real product UI screenshots, abstract gradient ribbons/meshes, flat-but-dimensional illustration with gradient fills, customer logos in original colors (homepage) or monochrome (classic strips)
design_style.ornamentation: restrained but present: dotted-grid corner ornaments (docs), diagonal cuts, aurora glows on navy, gradient hairline accents — every ornament uses the brand spectrum
design_style.whitespace_philosophy: generous but not extreme — denser than Vercel/Linear minimalism; Stripe fills sections with evidence (screenshots, stats, logos) rather than emptiness
design_style.copy_voice: assured, quantified, benefit-led ('Financial infrastructure to grow your revenue', 'from your first transaction to your billionth'); metrics as proof everywhere (1.68% of global GDP, 99.999% uptime); sentence-case headings; CTAs are short verb phrases with chevrons
design_style.interaction_feel: smooth and premium; ambient motion in the hero, quick conventional hovers elsewhere; live-updating numbers create a sense of a running machine

overview.effect_intensity: moderate — one signature WebGL gradient animation per marketing page, ambient not interactive
overview.performance_tier: heavy on marketing heroes (WebGL canvas, verified webgl context on both / and /payments), lightweight elsewhere (CSS/DOM); docs are effect-free static UI
overview.fallback_strategy: static gradient image where WebGL unavailable; content never depends on the canvas
overview.primary_technology: custom WebGL fragment-shader gradients on <canvas> (no Three.js/GSAP/PIXI globals detected)

webgl_gradient_canvas.details: THE Stripe signature. Homepage: 'hero-wave-animation__canvas' — a flowing 3D ribbon of orange→pink→violet→blue sweeping diagonally across the full hero, painterly and silk-like. /payments: 'Gradient__canvas isLoaded' — the classic animated mesh gradient (blurple/cyan/coral/magenta blobs slowly morphing), clipped by the diagonal hero edge. Both are continuous slow ambient loops, pointer-events none, behind content

gradient_text.details: homepage h1: first sentence solid navy, remainder background-clip gradient text whose colors animate through the brand spectrum; classic pages use static gradient text on select stats

live_data_liveness.details: 'Global GDP running on Stripe: 1.68460731%' ticker counts continuously in the hero; stat numerals (2x, 500M+) reinforce the running-machine feel

diagonal_clip_sections.details: classic pages: hero canvas and section bands clipped on a shallow diagonal (transform: skewY on background layers) — straight-edged on the new homepage

aurora_glow_on_dark.details: dark navy bands get a soft purple/blurple radial horizon glow (homepage 'backbone of global commerce' stats band) — a light source rising behind a dark horizon

floating_screenshot_compositions.details: product UI screenshots layered/overlapped at slight tilt with large violet-tinted shadows; docs tilt a 'test cards' credit-card illustration; blog hero cards carry full-bleed gradient artwork

scroll_effects.details: gentle fade/translate section reveals; no parallax, no scroll-scrubbing

dotted_grid_ornament.details: docs only: page corners carry a dot-grid pattern whose dots are tinted by the brand gradient spectrum, fading toward the content

3d_elements.note: the ribbon reads 3D but is a shader gradient, not geometry

glassmorphism.note: one rgba(255,255,255,0.65) overlay panel observed; no frosted-blur language

svg_animations.note: SVGs are static logos and icons

visual_effects.composite_notes: Stripe's formula: spend the entire effects budget on one breathtaking ambient WebGL gradient in the hero, then earn trust below it with static evidence — screenshots, stats, logos, terminals. Color discipline is the real trick: a 5-hue gradient spectrum that would be garish anywhere else stays premium because the rest of the page is navy-on-white with tiny doses of blurple.
